programme.title: Seinfeld S7E1
programme.desc: After breaking up with their girlfriends, Jerry and George worry that they're destined to spend their aging years as lonely old men. As a result, Jerry resolves to make up with Melanie, while George sets out to find Susan, the NBC executive he used to date. Meanwhile, when Elaine's sleep is disturbed by a barking dog living near her new apartment, Kramer offers to get Newman to take care the noisy pet once and for all. While Jerry hears Kramer's warning about the pitfalls of marriage, George doesn't. And inspired by his fond memories of their relationship, George heads to Susan's apartment where he proposes on the spot. After breaking the news to his incredulous parents, George arrives to tell Jerry the news. However, when he learns that Jerry's reunion with Melanie was cut short because of her annoying eating habits, George has second thoughts about his proposal to Susan. Although she's against killing it, Elaine does agree to let Kramer and Newman kidnap the annoying dog and joins them on a mission to relocate it far from home. But after taking a bite out of Kramer's shirt, the dog finds its way home, where a telltale tag leads to Kramer, Newman and Elaine's arrest. Finally, when his plans to join Jerry for a movie are derailed, George's concerns about marrying Susan grow, especially after he can't watch an exciting baseball game on TV.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E2
programme.desc: Although Elaine refuses to admit it, George's upcoming wedding to Susan has her troubled. And when a rabbi in her building senses something is wrong and encourages her to speak freely, she admits to feelings of jealousy and resentment. Meanwhile, George continues having second thoughts about Susan and seeks Jerry's advice on postponing the Christmas wedding until the first day of Spring. When George raises the subject with Susan, he immediately backs down after she tearfully suggests that he really doesn't want to get married at all. However, after witnessing another man refusing to succumb to a woman's tears, he resolves to take control of the situation and insist on the postponement. While picking up Elaine for a movie, Jerry runs into the rabbi, who freely reveals the details of her confession. But the discovery that her secrets have been disclosed gets Elaine out of the mood to be with her friends. Meanwhile, at the theater with Jerry, Kramer tries to smuggle in a hot cup of coffee underneath his shirt -- with disastrous results. When he demands the postponement and ends up crying, too, George finds that his own tears have convinced Susan to agree. Confronting the indiscreet rabbi, Elaine discovers that his revelations just stop with Jerry. Meanwhile, as the newly liberated George suddenly feels Susan's reins tightening once again, Kramer reveals that he's suing over the burns he suffered from the hot coffee. Finally, when they happen upon the rabbi's cable TV show, George and Susan are shocked to discover they are the focus of his sermon.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E3
programme.desc: While shopping for new clothes at a store owned by her uncle, Susan does her best to change George's wardrobe. But he's preoccupied by a store security guard who spends all day standing on the job. And when George comes back later, the guard admits that a chair would be nice for a change. Meanwhile, Kramer and his attorney Jackie Chiles are certain they'll win big in his lawsuit against Java World. But when his friend, a police orchestra conductor who prefers to be called maestro, stops in with a special balm for his burns, it works too well and jeopardizes the lawsuit. When Java World offers free coffee for life to settle the suit, Kramer hastily accepts, unaware that they were also prepared to pay him $50,000. Then, during a dinner date, the orchestra conductor persuades Elaine to call him maestro, too. Meanwhile after listening to him brag about his home in Tuscany, Jerry refuses to believe maestro's claim that there are no other houses available in the Italian region. So, when he invites Elaine to join him in Tuscany, Jerry is determined to prove he can find a place of his own there, too. Though his lawyer is humiliated by the quick settlement, Kramer is anxious to start cashing in. But all that free coffee soon starts affecting on his behavior. And when Jerry gets a lead on a house in Tuscany, he's unable to refuse the offer and ends up renting a place he didn't really want. Meanwhile, after George provides him with a new rocking chair, the guard falls asleep on the job and the store ends up being robbed. Finally, while at his villa, Elaine and maestro are shocked to see their new neighbors...Jerry and Kramer.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E4
programme.desc: Though it's grapefruit juice that has caused George to wink uncontrollably, Yankee executive Mr. Wilhelm thinks he's hinting at a problem with his boss, Mr. Morgan, and Kramer takes it as an okay to sell an autographed birthday card intended for the team's owner. Meanwhile, after Elaine agrees to date her wake- up service man, James, she reveals that her cousin Holly found Jerry's new diet a problem during a recent date. So when Jerry joins the two cousins for dinner, Elaine borrows his jacket for the walk home, unaware it's hiding his serving of mutton in the pockets wrapped in a pair of old napkins. But when Holly discovers their Grandma Mimma's antique napkins are missing, she's sure Elaine is a thief. Although Kramer has already sold the card to a memorabilia dealer, George must get it back for Morgan to sign so it can be presented to Yankee owner George Steinbrenner. Because it now belongs to a boy hospitalized with a fatal disease, Kramer can get it only by promising that Yankee Paul O'Neill will hit two home runs in the next game. Meanwhile, to escape a pack of mutton-hungry dogs, Elaine seeks refuge in James's apartment. However, her spending the night causes him to oversleep. And when James forgets to wake his newest client, Mr. Morgan's late arrival at work causes Wilhelm to be concerned. While Holly is cooking pork chops for Jerry, Elaine shows up to meet James. And when he arrives with Jerry's jacket, Holly is upset to sees James's dogs wearing Mimma's napkins. Finally, after claiming responsibility for the circumstances that reflected poorly on Morgan, George gets the card back. But because it's been framed and Morgan is unable to sign it, George not only gets credit for a job well done, but is promoted when his boss is fired.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E5
programme.desc: Elaine has agreed to put up a marathon runner who once overslept and missed an Olympic race. But when her track record for lateness gives Jean-Paul reason to think he's picked the wrong host, Jerry takes responsibility for getting him to the New York City Marathon on time. To keep from being bothered at work, George adopts an annoyed look. But it suggests to his boss that he's working too hard. So Mr. Wilhelm asks him to relax by entertaining some visiting Houston Astros executives, whose casual use of profanity George quickly adopts. Meanwhile, a malfunctioning pump in his new hot tub causes Kramer's body temperature to plummet. When Mr. Wilhelm overhears some off-color banter with his Astros counterparts, he worries that George has gone over the edge. And after the colorful language rubs off on Jean-Paul, his comments to a single mother and neighbor of Elaine's create entirely the wrong impression. Meanwhile, after persuading Jean-Paul to stay in a hotel on the eve of the race, Jerry's doubts about the desk clerk responsible for his wake-up call send a frustrated Jean-Paul back to the Seinfeld apartment. As she's being accused of telling Jean-Paul about the child her neighbor bore out of wedlock, Elaine is unaware that the nosy Rabbi Kirschbaum is listening nearby. As a result, not only does the entire building now know the neighbor's secret, but they've all weighed in on Elaine's supposed relationship with Jean-Paul, too. And even though Kramer's powerful new hot tub pump causes a blackout which disables Jerry's alarm clock, Jean-Paul makes to the starting line as the race is beginning. But just as Jean-Paul takes the lead, he accidentally mistakes Kramer's cup of scalding hot tea for water.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E6
programme.desc: While on the way to a soup stand whose demanding proprietor's harsh rules have led to him being referred to as "The Soup Nazi," Elaine stops to buy a sidewalk vendor's antique armoire. But when she's unable to move it into her building on a Sunday, she hires Kramer to watch it on the sidewalk overnight. Meanwhile, after George's question about bread causes the Soup Nazi to bar him from lunch, Elaine spots him in line trying again. While waiting, the two complain about Jerry's romantic behavior around his new girlfriend Sheila. And though George is successful in getting served this time, Elaine discovers just how tough the Soup Nazi can be when she's banned from eating there for two years. Kramer loses the armoire to a pair of sophisticated furniture thieves. And when a kiss in line costs Sheila a delicious bowl of soup, Jerry is prompted to claim he doesn't know her. Pointing out that he chose a bowl of soup over a woman, Elaine suggests he's acting even stranger than George. However, Jerry thinks it's George who's been acting strange, which puts the Seinfeld-Costanza friendship to the test. Meanwhile, the Soup Nazi gives Kramer an old armoire he's been keeping in his basement. Upon finding him making amends with Sheila, George decides to make a point about Jerry's romantic antics. But when he tries using his own silly terms of endearment with his fiancee Susan, George doesn't anticipate just how much she'll like it. Meanwhile, when her thank you for the armoire enrages the Soup Nazi, Elaine is determined to get revenge for his rudeness. And the recipes she's found hidden in his old armoire provide just the ammunition. Finally, as Jerry announces he's breaking up with Sheila after all, the Soup Nazi closes up shop for good, much to the dismay of his loyal customers.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E7
programme.desc: While George balks at giving his secret bank code to Susan, Jerry is hired to do a commercial for appliance dealer Leapin' Larry. However, when Jerry's foot falls asleep during their first meeting, the one-legged Larry is sure he's being cruelly mocked. And when Jerry's friend Fred doesn't remember her from a party, Elaine wants to know why, while Kramer's fascination with firefighters leads him to buy an emergency scanner. Meanwhile, in hopes of salvaging an evening with Elaine and her boss, clothing catalog legend J. Peterman, Jerry tricks George into joining them. When Elaine gets another offer from Fred, she calls Peterman to cancel...but not Jerry and George. So when Jerry comes up with an excuse, too, George ends up at a hospital with Peterman's dying mother, where his bank code becomes her final word. Stranded by Elaine once again at the funeral, George is left with Peterman, who's intent on uncovering the meaning behind his mother's dying words. Meanwhile, after apologizing to Leapin' Larry, Jerry accidentally sets his appliance store ablaze and unknowingly helps Kramer realize a lifelong dream of driving a hook and ladder truck. Told of a fire at Leapin' Larry's, Peterman takes George to see if they can help. Spotting someone with his sleeve stuck the burning building's automated teller machine, Peterman demands George's secret bank code to help rescue the endangered man. And upon learning that the code and his mother's dying words are one and the same, he accuses George of killing her. Finally, other than satisfying her curiosity, Elaine realizes that dating Fred was a big mistake.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E8
programme.desc: When Elaine has an extra ticket to a museum's costume show, Jerry suggests asking Susan. However, worried about mixing his life with Susan with that of his friends, George tries to keep it from happening. While at the movies with Kramer, Jerry runs into the recently fired pool man at his health club. And though Jerry tries discouraging him, Ramon ends up joining them for the evening. Meanwhile, when his new phone number bring a flurry of wrong number calls for Movie Phone, Kramer takes it upon himself to help as many callers as he can. Uninterested in his friendship, Jerry tries to let Ramon down gently. But Ramon assumes that it's because he cleans pools for a living, and getting his job back at the club provides the perfect opportunity for revenge. Yet, when Jerry tries to even the score, he nearly kills Ramon while getting himself and Newman banished from the club. After refusing to join them at the coffee shop, George is furious to learn Susan is at the movies with Jerry and Elaine. So he calls Movie Phone to find out where he can find them to put an end to their developing friendship. But he gets Kramer instead. And when George's impatience sends him to the wrong theater, he's thrown out for creating a disturbance. However, he needn't have bothered since Susan already decided she's ill-suited for Jerry and Elaine's company.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E9
programme.desc: While sponsoring Kramer for an AIDS Walk, Jerry spots the name of an old college classmate on the sign-up sheet. Over Elaine's objections, he copies down Lena Small's phone number and calls her for a date. Worried that Lena would mind, Jerry then keeps secret just how he got the number until he's forced to tell George in confidence. But an argument over secrecy prompts George to tell Susan everything, including Jerry's secret. Meanwhile, when her favorite birth control device, the Today Sponge, is taken off the market, Elaine searches high and low until she finds the last remaining case. When Susan sends him to pick up some birth control so they can make up after their fight, George discovers she uses the sponge, too. Knowing Elaine has the last remaining case, he heads for her apartment. But after refusing George's request to borrow one, Elaine also has second thoughts about sex with her boyfriend Billy for fear of depleting her precious supply. Meanwhile, Jerry fears are confirmed when Susan starts a chain of phone calls which eventually lead to Lena hearing how he got her number. And Kramer's refusal to wear an AIDS Ribbon makes him a pariah among his fellow walkers. Though George claims not to like them, Susan persuades him to use a condom for their "make-up sex." But the pressure of opening the wrapper proves too daunting, leaving them unsatisfied. Then, after subjecting Billy to some rigorous questioning, Elaine decides to use one, but only one, of her sponges on him. Meanwhile, when Lena is okay with how he got her phone number, Jerry begins to think she may be too good for him. However, discovering her own large supply of sponges cause him to reconsider. But it's Jerry's other little secret -- changing the size on the label of his jeans to make them appear smaller -- that finally turns Lena off.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E10
programme.desc: To help revive an old movie theater, Kramer calls on George's childhood neighbor Lloyd Braun, who's recovering from a nervous breakdown. Gloating over Lloyd's misfortune, George runs into his old neighbor Pop Lazzari. But when his daughter Deena reveals he gave up his garage after a nervous breakdown of his own, it's too late for him to keep Pop from working on George's car. Uncomfortable about sitting with Lloyd at the theater, Elaine claims Jerry forgot his glasses and asks to be put in the front row. And to protect Lloyd's feelings, Kramer gives Jerry a pair of glasses from the theater's lost and found. But when Kramer learns they belong to the guest of honor at the theater's grand reopening, he trades them for a much stronger pair, making it virtually impossible for Jerry to see. Meanwhile, a pair of revealing accidents convince Lloyd that Elaine is coming on to him. Though upset with the noises now coming from his car, George is excited when Deena suggests they get together --- until discovering she thinks he's on the verge of a nervous breakdown. And listening to George blame everyone from her father to a coffee shop cashier for a fire that destroyed his car only reinforces the belief. Yet, it's seeing George promoting a theater matinee dressed as King Henry the Eighth that convinces Deena he's insane.

programme.title: Seinfeld S7E11
programme.desc: With her new relationship with jazz musician John Germaine going well, Elaine lets Jerry in on the news. However, when Jerry then mentions it to a member of John's band, she worries about appearing too eager. Meanwhile, wanting to make a good impression, George's dad insists on bringing a special loaf of rye bread to a dinner with Susan's parents. And after a buying spree at a bulk discount store, Kramer agrees to drive a friend's hansom cab for a week. While returning home from their disastrous dinner, George discovers his dad kept the loaf of marble rye they brought as a gift. While George insists it was just an oversight, Frank and Estelle are sure the Rosses didn't serve the bread on purpose. And when it's found to be missing, Mr. Ross is certain that the Costanzas took it back. Meanwhile, her next date convinces Elaine that Jerry's remarks have caused John to cool towards her. When George decides to secretly replace the bread, he hires Kramer to give Mr. & Mrs. Ross a hansom cab ride for their wedding anniversary and sends Jerry to buy a replacement loaf. However, Jerry's arrival with the bread is delayed when he must fight an elderly customer for the bakery's last loaf. And Kramer's horse's extreme case of flatulence causes the Ross's cab ride to be cut short. So, upon returning home early, they catch George and Jerry trying to sneak the rye bread inside. Finally, after Elaine admits she's interested in him, their quick trip to John's apartment ends up jeopardizing his career.

programme.title: H2O : Just add Water, Season 2
programme.desc: Cleo notes that Don seems to be down since her mother left. Later, Lewis finds an old book on mermaids and tests out a potion recipe on the girls, supposed to grant wishes. Cleo instantly thinks it could help get Don and her Mum back together. They make the potion and it doesn't seem to work.

programme.title: H2O : Just add Water, Season 2
programme.desc: Life isn't the same at Cleo's house since her mother left. When it appears that Don has a girlfriend - Cleo and Kim are horrified, thinking this means a step-mother. Meanwhile, Charlotte is making more moves on Lewis, making Cleo increasingly jealous. Don organises a dinner and Cleo is shocked to find her Dad's new partner is Charlotte's mother!

programme.title: H2O : Just add Water, Season 2
programme.desc: Cleo has been getting into some trouble with her boss Mrs Geddes at work and when Ronnie the dolphin splashes her, Cleo laughs and is fired. At the same time, Lewis finds himself in desperate need of a job after his laptop dies and, unbeknownst to him, he is offered Cleo's old job. Upset, the girls use their mermaid powers to make the job unbearable for Lewis.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1
programme.desc: It's a month since Tara's injury and her plaster is due to come off but Tara seems much more focused on her new boyfriend, Christian, and organising the school formal. Sammy and Abigail decide take their relationship to the next level.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1
programme.desc: Christian's sentencing hearing has been set and, believing that he's headed for juvenile detention, Christian decides to stop playing by other people's rules and goes back to his old neighbourhood.

programme.title: Mako Mermaids
programme.desc: Ondina blames Weilan for the dragon taking Evie's tail. Feeling responsible, Weilan considers leaving Mako, but some sage advice from Uncle Shen helps her reconsider.

programme.title: Mako Mermaids
programme.desc: When Zac discovers a magical seashell that makes wishes come true, he accidentally sends the mermaids back in time.

programme.title: Mako Mermaids
programme.desc: During a lesson on Mako Island, one of Ondina's little mermaid students, Amaris, goes missing. Chris and Karl come to the island to survey the turtle population.

programme.title: The Elephant Princess, Season 1
programme.desc: Kuru reports diplomatically to Omar about Alex's slow progress and Devi to Vashan that she's still a magically clumsy brat living a commoner's life, the rival prince allows her to return as butterfly and seduce Kuru as Juliet, so he'll cease being her only good influence.

programme.title: Heirs of the Night, Season 1
programme.desc: The Conte and Contessa do not want to believe Dracula is alive. He has been captured. Since Dracula and Alisa are connected by the Spark Alisa gets trapped into a coma. Lars is the only one who has the power to help Alisa. As Alisa awakes, she feels that most of the special powers have left her body. But she is alive, thanks to Lars.

programme.title: Wolfblood
programme.desc: Jana makes the shock decision that this will be her last full moon in the city. While the pack wrestle with the disappointment of losing their alpha, TJ falls for the smoke and mirrors of movie producer Joshua Hartington. Meanwhile Katrina attempts to train ex-Segolia worker Robyn.

programme.title: Wolfblood
programme.desc: The mystery wolfblood is unmasked and brings startling news from the Wild Pack. Jana summons the strength to help locate TJ, but her interference backfires with devastating consequences.

programme.title: H2O
programme.desc: Why has a whale calf run aground on an isolated beach near Dolphin City? And what's causing the girls' migraines? Does it have anything to do with the dolphin twins' bizarre sickness? During their investigations the mermaids come across a strange fishing boat using illegal technology: an extremely low-frequency military radar device.

programme.title: Lightening Point
programme.desc: ZOEY is sick of waiting and decides to take charge of finding a way home. Back at the cane field, ZOEY and AMBER test out the energy field one more time.

programme.desc: Designers team up with a hairstylist for a head-to-toe fashion collaboration.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: Designers must create editorial looks inspired by renowned model Coco Rocha—without a runway!

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: Designers give everything they've got for a chance to make a collection for New York Fashion Week.

programme.title: Project Runway

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: Fourteen of the most popular designers from Project Runway descend on New York to try for the coveted title that has eluded them. But the fun and games end when judge Nina Garcia and Christian Siriano arrive to give them an unwanted history lesson…

programme.title: Project Runway

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: A couple fall in love with a converted stable block in North Yorkshire but will it be enduring love after all the work is done? And projects get underway in Stoke and Tunbridge Wells.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a tired looking bungalow in County Durham bought by a couple with three Labradors and meets a new property developer who is working on a terraced house in Wales while Lucy follows the progress of a flat in central London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: A ground floor flat, an end terraced house and a bungalow, all in need of modernisation, are sold under the hammer but will the new owners make any money on their investments? Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander have a look round and check up on progress.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: A substantial Kent property divided into flats and a three bedroom house, small workshops in Derbyshire and a one bedroom flat in a leafy Surrey town are all sold at auction, all needing work done on them if their new owners are going to make any money.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: A Cornish farmhouse with stunning coastal views, a garden flat in Essex and a grade two listed Georgian house in Kent all go under the Hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners and hear their renovation plans.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Today we meet a father and son team with high end plans for a Surrey bungalow, two friends who plan to renovate a one bedroom flat in Gateshead and a man with lofty ambitions to convert an Edwardian water tower into a home.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: A piece of land in Kent, a terraced house in South Wales and a flat with a quirky layout in the borders of Scotland all go under the Hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners but will any of them manage to make money on their investments?

programme.title: Sporting Heroes

programme.title: Adventure Ocean Quest
programme.desc: Adventure Ocean Quest - Season 1 - Shark Paradise of Polynesia: French Polynesia's great hammerhead sharks are extraordinarily shy animals and still pose many questions, not least where they go during their annual migration. Freediver Frederic Buyle and cameraman Christian Petron team up with scientist Dr. Johan Mourir to investigate these bizarre predators.

programme.title: Adventure Ocean Quest
programme.desc: Adventure Ocean Quest - Season 1 - The Giants of Rurutu: Humpback whales have come back from the brink of extinction, but they are still difficult to approach in the water and their behaviour and vocalisations pose many unsolved questions. Dr. Michael Poole wants to find out if the whales respond differently to a silent freediver compared to conventional divers, and has asked Frederic to dive with the whales and observe them.

programme.title: Font of Life
programme.desc: Starting at Imbak Falls, wildlife photographer Cede Prudente together with boatman Rizal start their journey down Borneo's longest river ­ the Kinabatangan. Cede is on a mission to photograph the varied wildlife along this river from source to sea. In this episode, we meet some modern day Dusun people, an ethnic group once notorious as headhunters.

programme.title: How the Greeks Changed the World
programme.desc: The Ascent of Civilization is a gripping landmark series with a twist – a fresh look at three ancient trailblazer cultures that have shaped all of our lives. Democracy, science, theatre, plumbing, modern roads, laws, the Olympics, conquering the seas, the discovery of America, and the onset of globalisation … you'd be hard-pushed to find a more eclectic list of achievements. They're all at the heart of modern society, but their origins were passed down to us through the ages. It's an inheritance that leads us back to the world of the Ancients – the world of the Greeks, the Romans and the Vikings.

programme.title: The First Victim
programme.desc: Hitler's Poland Campaign unleashed a “Blitzkrieg” lasting six weeks only. However, it was a war of unprecedented brutality resulting in a tremendous suffering of the Polish people in the long run. Jews were herded into Ghettos other residents were imprisoned in camps for forced labor or displaced to make way for German settlers. Hundreds and thousands of them became victims of the Nazi murder machine. In part 2, “World War II” sheds light on some of their lives telling almost forgotten stories.

programme.title: The Silicon Valley Revolution
programme.sub-title: How a few nerds did change the world
programme.desc: The Silicon Valley Revolution - How a few nerds did change the world - From the first Computer Trade Fair to the WWW: The dream of personal computers slowly turns into Big Business. The dream of a few leftist visionaries took over the world in the 1980s: the now legendary Macintosh goes on sale, Bill Gates creates the foundation for his economic imperium, the world wide web connects billions of people all over the world, Google is founded.

programme.title: The Human Limits
programme.desc: The Human Limits - Season 1 - Masters of the Mind: In this part, six talents prove their extraordinary abilities and show how they control their mind. There's Lukas Irmler who's slacklining in dizzying heights above the abyss, Shaolin monk Shi Yan Yao who has spent his whole life practicing meditation and can bend a steel bar with his neck and mathematical super brains Gert Mittring and Rüdiger Gramm who can solve nearly every arithmetic problem without a pocket calculator.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Investigate released documents regarding alien and UFO encounters, made accessible to the public in 2011 by the Freedom of Information Act. We investigate mass UFO sightings, personal abductions, government cover-ups and breaking alien news from around the world. We delve into the "photoshopping" of space, the value of Wikileaks, and the role social media plays in alien stories.

programme.title: Bucketlist Europe
programme.desc: Europe is a densely packed continent, with some 700 million residents living across 50 countries. It's famed for its ancient cities and civilisations, but is sometimes overlooked when it comes to the natural world. But there's no doubt that the continent is a treasure trove of natural beauty, a mosaic of landscapes each filled with their own unique animal communities. If you were planning a bucket list trip of the natural wonders of Europe, you'd only have to ask yourself one question: where would you start?

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: The vet students meet for the first time and travel 7 hours to the remote clinic. We learn about their fears and insecurities -- fear of failure and the weight of responsibilities ahead.

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: The vets get situated in their dormitory accommodations. Dr. Greg deliberately pairs them according to their differences. Vets need to learn to work with colleagues and clients different from themselves.

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest
programme.desc: All habitats present difficulties, but the challenges of the desert are more obvious than most – scorching heat and a lack food and water make them one of the most hostile environments on the planet. But with great challenges come incredible solutions. Despite the deadly conditions, a hardy band of unique animal and plant species have adapted to meet the inhospitable desert conditions head on. Each one finds its own way to exploit and conserve the available food and water in a daily struggle for survival.

programme.title: Animal Record Breakers
programme.desc: We love to measure aspects of life at its extremes. The biggest, the tallest, even the rarest – some of the most iconic creatures in the natural world. Many are familiar but it's also important to recognise some of the more surprising record breakers.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.desc: Cape Verde – the islands of Saudade, of longing. For Stéphane and Richard it was a journey full of adventure, unforgettable encounters and magnificent photographs. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied them on their trip.

programme.title: Wild Bavaria
programme.desc: The moon disappears behind the horizon and the stage is set for a new day in the Alps of Berchtesgaden.

programme.title: Wild Bavaria
programme.desc: Mighty and mysterious, King Watzmann sits above Germany's only alpine national park.

programme.title: Bavaria's Alpine Kingdom
programme.desc: There are sparkling palaces from ice and snow, like those found in a fairytale. The icy castles are the phenomenal work of Mother Nature but man had a hand in the magnificent stone castles of the region. The spectacular and histrionic mountains of the Bavarian Alps challenge wildlife and the people who live there and the rocky ramparts of this mountain kingdom are built of limestone and fossil rocks that tell of its origins beneath the sea. Millions of visitors from all over the world come to Bavaria every year to witness the mysticism of the landscape. Kings and Queens, tourist and locals, all are welcomed in the fantastic alpine kingdom. Early summer often comes with the threat of storms that bring floods that scour the land and threaten many creatures. But farmers risk these dangers to take their herds to the highest pastures, where they can graze on the sweetest flower-filled meadows. And later from the high-quality milk produced by their herds, the famers make highly prized cheese. Humankind lives among eagle owls, chamoix, wild cats, stoats and ravens up in these mountains.

programme.title: Treasure of Lima
programme.desc: This is a story about a remote island in the pacific.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.desc: I'm in a destination that is easily considered to be one of the most beautiful in the world. A place of immense natural beauty that helps create a renewed connection to nature. Here's why Sonoma County is a place to love.

programme.title: Samantha Browns Places to Love
programme.desc: There's nothing quite like a destination that knows how to embrace the cold. Samantha loves winter, and she's always looking for places that love it just as much. Join her as she explores the many reasons why Madison, Wisconsin is a Place to Love.

programme.title: Samantha Browns Places to Love
programme.desc: Welcome to Big Sky, Montana! This is a destination that sees 400 inches of powder on average and is home to nearly 6,000 skiable acres. No matter the season or how low the temperatures drops, there's always something inspiring to find if you're curious enough to look. From embracing the snowy outdoors to enjoying the local businesses of a tight knit community, join Samantha as she explores what makes Big Sky a Place to Love.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: My Van Life
programme.desc: Lisa meets off-grid desert nomads turning to this lifestyle out of necessity rather than choice. Van Life rebels rule in Slab City, while in Nevada, Bob Wells - the godfather of Van Life - helps thousands turn to nomadic life when in dire straits.

programme.title: Back Home

programme.title: Back Home

programme.title: Animal Sounds
programme.desc: This show blends fun and learning by introducing kids to a musical world where animals take center stage. With catchy tunes, children explore songs about pets and wildlife, fostering care and empathy while enjoying an universe of music and adventure!

programme.title: Let's Play Everyday
programme.desc: This show features nursery songs perfect for any moment, offering a fun and engaging soundtrack for kids' daily adventures. These entertaining and educational tunes captivate young audiences, sparking joy and learning, no matter the activity!

programme.title: Dance and Freeze
programme.desc: This lively show is perfect for energetic kids, blending fun and fitness through animated characters and dance moves. It promotes active participation, keeping kids entertained and giving them a great workout.

programme.title: Sweet Dreams
programme.desc: "Sweet Dreams" is a soothing show with gentle lullabies and serene animations. It helps kids relax and fall asleep with ease, making bedtime an enjoyable and comforting experience for the whole family.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are in Home Hill making loud footstep noises, but Tinky Winky has spilt Tubby Custard on his foot. Who should clean it up? A group of children go for a walk in the woods to play with the autumn leaves.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies love to watch children. Today, they see Ella and Lilly who tell us about their dog - called Alice. A dog appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po say's "Eh-oh" and runs off to join the other Tubbies when they watch Andy Brown and some children go on a boat trip to feed some sealions. The Tubbies are very busy at the switch panel, pressing buttons and pushing levers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Andy Brown and some children visit some cygnets. In Teletubbyland, it's time for the Tubbies to go to bed, but Po is not tired, so she rides her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies love to run round and round Teletubbyland, but stop to watch three children make some modelling dough to play with. The Tubby Toaster makes a big piece of Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A balloon appears in Teletubbyland it floats in the air. The Teletubbies follow the balloon all over Teletubbyland, but they can't catch it.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po make some adjustments on the switch panel and laugh at the funny noises. The Tubbies watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty duck. There's time for a dance before Tubby Bye-Bye.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A magic watering can appears in Teletubbyland, and Po has fun watering everything and watching them grow. The Teletubbies watch two children make ice cream sundaes with jelly.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies run out of the house to find their favourite things. Then they run off to watch some children flying kites. A special cloud falls on Tinky Winky's head.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch as Andy Brown shows some children how butterflies are. The Teletubbies love flying their kites and a strong wind blows Po all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky calls the other Tubbies to come and watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story about the naughty sock. The Tubby Toast machine makes a tower of tubby toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind a hill to watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty sausage. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po go for a walk, so Laa-Laa decides to dance a secret dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance an up and down dance. They watch some children painting pictures and patterns on to eggs. In Teletubbyland, Laa-Laa dances with her ball until the magical tree appears.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: There's someone...or something strange in the neighborhood and the kids imagine the worst - but Strawberry finds it's a new girl, Blueberry Muffin.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Strawberryland's one movie theatre goes out of business, Strawberry has an idea to save it: she and her friends can film a movie to show there and attract crowds back to the theatre.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Where, Oh, Where Has Our Blueberry Gone?
programme.desc: Blueberry has become obsessed with the mystery book detective heroine, Patti Persimmon. She decorates her store with Patti posters and rearranges her shelves to make room for all the Patti books she's ordered. Blueberry begins to neglect outings with her friends and even goes so far as to dress like her idol just to emulate her. She then starts looking for cases to solve around town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While in the midst of a cake decorating demonstration at the café, the girls are uncharacteristically interrupted by an excited Princess Berrykin. She announces the Berrykins have decided to revive the old tradition of the Berry Bitty Buggy Derby. While the girls enthusiastically recount the stories they've heard about it and ask the Princess questions, Blueberry, carrying enough books to block her view, comes through the door and unknowingly interrupts the Princess.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry's friend Coco Calypso lives on Seaberry Beach with her pet, Papaya Parrot, and a band of fun-loving monkeys.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry Shortcake is planning a first birthday party for her sister Apple Dumpling, so she sets off on an adventure to find the perfect party supplies.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's a retelling of "Sleeping Beauty" - but our focus is on the fairy that didn't get her invitation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: The engines on the Island of Sodor are excited! A photographer has arrived to take photographs for a brand new book: Sodor - The Great Railway!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: It's Thomas' turn to work with his good friend Whiff the Rubbish Engine! He has to be Whiff's back engine and push a very heavy rubbish train.

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: At the farm, Farmer McColl needs help. His scarecrow is old and has to go to be mended. This means there is nothing to scare away the crows and Farmer McColl doesn't know what to do!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: Gordon the Grand Engine doesn't feel so grand when he bashes into some cars at the Docks - and his buffer falls off!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: Percy and Diesel have been given a very unusual job! They must pick up a very heavy and very big, old organ: a calliope.

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: A famous composer is visiting the Island of Sodor to write a piece of music about the Island. Thomas is very excited and very proud that he has been chosen to take the famous composer on a tour!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: It's Christmas time on Sodor and Edward is with Salty at the Docks. Edward's excited. Christmas time is present time!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: On the Island of Sodor, it's Sodor Surprise Day! This is the day when everyone on Sodor has fun surprising each other and making them laugh!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: It's Christmas time on Sodor and there's been a disaster! Sir Topham Hatt has lost his top hat! Emily is given the very important job of finding it.

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: It is the day of the Sodor Farmer's Fair! There's a lot to do for all the engines. James is given the important job of taking Farmer McColl's sheep to the Fair.

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: At the Docks, Whiff looks in wonder at the mighty Spencer. The Mayor tells Spencer that he is fast, strong, and loud which makes Spencer a very grand engine indeed!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: There is great excitement on Sodor. Stafford, the new shunting engine, is at the Steamworks. He's electric and runs on batteries, so everyone wants to meet him!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: It's Christmas and at the Blue Mountain Quarry, Rheneas hears that Thomas is going to collect a Christmas tree from Misty Island!

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: Up in the hills, on the Narrow Gauge Railway, Peter Sam is excited. The Thin Controller has to go down for a meeting with Sir Topham Hatt. He has asked Peter Sam to be in charge while he is away.

programme.title: Thomas and Friends
programme.desc: It's Sir Topham Hatt's birthday! He introduces Thomas to Winston - Sir Topham Hatt's new track inspection vehicle.

programme.title: Weekend At Halberts
programme.desc: Party time in Knighton! The mood is down so the King decides to throw a party. In the meantime Jestro attacks the Fortrex, but he is confronted with the new NEXO Power "The Wedgie of Doom". Clay is turned to stone and everyone is gloomy. Especially King Halbert. In order to get everything in a better mood Aaron organizes a celebration. The King wants a reenactment of Clay's heroic rescue of the Queen. He is (as usual) totally unaware of Clay's situation. In order not to disappoint him further, the Knights get a remote control for Clay's Battle suit and is thus able to “perform” as Clay. Lance is happy to exercise his acting abilities and quickly gets into the role of the uptight knight of knights. Ava is working on reinstalling Merlok 2.0 into the Fortrex with Robin. She is also preparing the Fortrex for a visit from the new Knights' Academy novices. It is a fieldtrip with Principal Brickland and Sir Griffiths with the goal of teaching the kids about these new so-called High-tech battle strategies. Not really feeling it for anything modern, it quickly turns into a regular trip back memory lane for Brickland and Sir Griffiths. This out of control excursion is an excellent moment for Jestro who is looking for an opportunity to cheer up the defeated Monstrox. Unfortunately, for Jestro, Ava is able to make Merlok 2.0 functional again, and the Novices are able to fight Jestro and his monsters off using the NEXO Power: Wedgie of Doom. It is a humiliating defeat. Apparently, this is just what the doctor ordered for Monstrox. A good laugh at someone else's expense. His evil mood is back on.

programme.title: Awakening
programme.desc: When Mateo's comic creation, Z-Blob, follows him from a dream back to Brooklyn, Mateo and his sister realize the Dream World is real, and in danger!

programme.title: Dream Chasers
programme.desc: After a daring mission to save their friend in the Dream World, Mateo and Izzie find the nightmare has followed them back to the Waking World.

programme.title: Legacy, Vehicle and Mechs
programme.desc: Zane takes help from Nya for backup memories of their adventures to the Samurai X Cave computer, but Zane encounters a mysterious puzzle buried deep within his code. Nya digitizes Jay and uploads him into Zane's mind. Jay reviews memories of the Ninja's various motorcycles, mechs and planes in order to solve the vehicle-themed puzzle.

programme.title: The Oni And The Dragon
programme.desc: While Princess Harumi learns to adjust to the life of a Ninja, Lloyd and Jay search for answers about the Oni Masks at Mystake's Tea house, while Cole and Zane attempt to infiltrate the Sons of Garmadon biker gang. Remembering that Lloyd managed to arrest one of the gang members at Borg Tower, the Ninja decide to interrogate him to ascertain the location of the third Oni Mask. In order to get him to talk, however, Cole suggests they visit MYSTAKE'S TEA HOUSE to obtain some "truth tea." The truth-tea backfires spectacularly when Cole accidentally switches cups and drinks the tea himself. Zane is able to salvage the situation, however, and obtains a lead. He learns that the Sons of Garmadon frequent a karaoke bar called LAUGHY'S. Zane and Cole don disguises to infiltrate the bar. Cole assumes the alter ego “Rocky Dangerbuff," while Zane adopts the subtler moniker "Snake Jaguar." In order to earn the gang's trust, Cole performs a karaoke routine that is a smash success. Unfortunately, during the routine, his disguise comes loose and he is exposed as a ninja. The Sons of Garmadon attack him, and Zane (who is still in disguise) is unable to intervene. Cole is taken prisoner while Zane is invited back to the Sons of Garmadon's secret base.

programme.title: The Pranks
programme.desc: The friends are using their talents to pull a prank on Olivia. But who gets the last laugh? Stephanie finds the perfect front page story for the school paper! It's almost too good to be true. Emma and Andrea go undercover to solve the mystery of the two Jacobs.

programme.title: Spooky Sensation
programme.desc: The girls decided to spend a weekend at the hotel. It appears to be haunted. Watch the full video to know more about this.

programme.title: Sings of Love
programme.desc: See how Stephanie and Olivia enter the Under the Sea photo competition. Jacob wrote a love song! Olivia and her friends are very excited to hear. Tanya and Stephanie are teamed up in baking class. Watch the video for more fun.

programme.title: Video Wonder
programme.desc: Olivia and Andrea visited science camp with Mrs Stevens. On the other hand, Andrea and Mia decides to return a lost skunk to nature. Andrea was reading intimate postcards between Chef Pierre and Mrs. Stevens.

programme.title: Stiix and Stones
programme.desc: The Ninja travel to the Village of Stiix to find the thief who stole the scroll of Airjitzu, but the self-serving Ronan isn't keen to sell it cheaply so the Ninja must figure out a way to make money on the docks – and when they fail to make enough cash, the Ninja decide to steal it – but have a run in with Moro and Sowlarcha. The Ninja travel to the Village of Stiix to find the thief who stole the scroll of Airjitzu, but when the self-serving Ronan isn't keen to sell it cheaply, the Ninja must figure out a way to make money on the docks. Meanwhile, reluctant to give up being Samurai X, Nya struggles to embrace being a Water Ninja, and Sensei Wu gives her a riddle to figure out. Back at Stiix, when the Ninja fail to make enough cash working the docks, the Ninja decide to steal it... but have a run in with Moro and Sowlarcha. After Moro gets away with the scroll, the Ninja are left dangling, but Ronan saves them at a price – for a piece of their share in the Tea Farm.

programme.title: Public Enemy Number One
programme.desc: The Ninja must find out as much as they can about Nadakhan before he finds the Realm Crystal to save his crew, but after the Ninja are labeled enemies by the world they used to protect, our heroes find themselves hunted by their old friend, Ronan. With the Destiny's Bounty impounded, the police hire Ronin to track down the fugitive ninja. Misako has been arrested and is confronted by Nadakhan, who reveals he has trapped Wu in the Teapot of Tyrahn. Misako accidentally reveals the location of the Realm Crystal. The ninja discover that Wu was researching djinn when he disappeared and split up to learn more about their potential new foe. Lloyd and Zane head to the Library of Domu. While there, Ronin hacks into Zane's systems and shuts him down, then captures Lloyd. Ronin uses Zane's SOS beacon to lure Kai and Nya into the desert, where they are captured. Later, Cole and Jay are defeated by Ronin as well. Meanwhile, Nadakhan has used his powers to foil the high-tech security guarding the Realm Crystal and steals it. The ninja are hauled off to Kryptarium Prison and Nadakhan is free to gather his lost crew.

programme.title: Enkrypted
programme.desc: The secret, he says, is the venom of a Tiger Widow, a rare creature that lives on only one island. The venom can slow a djinn down. Soto is the only one who knows where the island is and will only tell if the ninja break him out of jail. Meanwhile, Nadakhan has decided to return to his home realm of Djinjago, only to discover it is falling apart. The ninja's destruction of the Cursed Realm is affecting the realms connected to it. Nadakhan uses the Djinn Blade to smash the Teapot, absorbing all the spirits that were trapped there into the sword. Falsely imprisoned, the Ninja are at their lowest point, but find an unlikely friend in an old enemy. Meanwhile, Nadakhan rescues his marooned crew and gains a new perspective, along with a new mysterious plan. With the ninja imprisoned, Nadakhan uses the Realm Crystal to free his old crew. The ninja encounter Captain Soto, the first captain of the Destiny's Bounty, who tells them the story of how he originally trapped Nadakhan in the Teapot. Nadakhan vows revenge on the ninja and NINJAGO. The ninja and Soto escape from jail, only to find out the map to the island is on Nadakhan's ship. Nadakhan discovers that Nya looks just like Delara, his long-lost love.

programme.title: Misfortune Rising
programme.desc: The Ninja are on a collision course to find the map to stop Nadakhan, they first must find him. Meanwhile, Jay makes a wish to change his future, but it could alter his past indefinitely. The ninja's search for Nadakhan's ship turns up nothing. Nadakhan's crew is struggling to handle an airship which used to sail only on the oceans. Nadakhan confronts Jay and talks him into making a wish that will win him Nya. Jay wishes that he wasn't born in a junkyard and was rich. The next moment, he receives a letter saying his father has died and he has inherited his estate. Jay rushes home to find his parents in the junkyard and finds out they are fine, but they reveal that he is adopted. Jay discovers that his real father is the actor who played his favorite movie hero. In an effort to trap the ninja, the Sky Pirates start raids all over NINJAGO City. Nadakhan kidnaps Kai, who makes two wishes, both of which go wrong. His third wish is to “make it all go away.” Nadakhan's first mate, Flintlocke, accidentally destroys the Realm Crystal and Cole steals Soto's map. The Destiny's Bounty mysteriously vanishes from the police impound yard.

programme.title: The Cloud
programme.desc: A storm is brewing over Knighton. Monstrox has taken the form of a cloud, and is raising an army of lightning infused Stone Monsters. His first target is the new Lavalands Amusement Park. Harmony reigns in Knighton as the Knights pursue their civilian lives: Lance has a TV talk show Axl is the singing chef on the show Jestro is the weatherman Clay is teaching at the Knights' Academy Macy, Ava and Merlok 2.0 travel the land in the Fortrex making sure the kingdom's defenses are ready (part of a plan she and Clay have) Aaron and his hover shield is travelling the land setting extreme records. Robin is at his workshop at the Knights' Academy doing an “independent study” – he's working on some top secret project for Merlok 2.0. It all seems excellent! We even see the Knights new vehicles – which have been optimized for civilian life (except for Clay's.) But storm clouds are brewing. Well, one storm cloud is brewing: the Cloud of Monstrox! Yes, the Book of Monsters has reconstituted himself and is now a cloud who needs a man on the ground to enact his new evil plan. He follows his old buddy Jestro as he goes across the realm on the weekends apologizing for being evil. ZAP! With one bolt of his evil magic lighting, the Cloud of Monstrox turns Jestro evil again (only this time a bit, well, too evil.) The evil duo are back together – and bring stone to life for the first time in the form of Gargoyles from the town of GHOULIA. Now they must gather the Forbidden Powers so that the Cloud of Monstrox can destroy the entire kingdom! A battle breaks out in the Lava Lands, where Lance and Alice Squires are reporting from the opening of the Cuthbert Richmond funded Lava Lands Amusement Park, rebuilt from the remains of Jestro's old HQ. Jestro and the Cloud are after something in the remains and as Merlok 2.0 sends out a call for the Knights to help Lance. Can Lance (with an assist from King Halbert in his Mech) hold-off Jestro, the Cloud and their new army of stone fighters?!

programme.title: A Little Rusty
programme.desc: The Cloud of Monstrox and Jestro unlocks a mysterious Forbidden Power. What kind ofhavoc will they unleash? And can Merlok 2.0 and the NEXO KNIGHTS make the new COMBO NEXO Powers work? The Knights finally make it to the Lava Lands – using their various new forms of transport, which are all strictly civilian vechicles – and they must regroup and learn to fight together again to face the new threat of Jestro, the Cloud of Monstrox, and their STONE MONSTERS (in this case, Stone Gargoyles.) But Jestro and his monsters beat back the knights and recover the STAFF OF MONSTROX – which was buried under the ceremonial chamber from Episode 210 (and is now the Lava Puker amusement park ride) – and they get away on Jestro's EVIL MOBILE, which he recovers from the HQ garage. Macy says this is exactly why she's been touring the kingdom preparing everyone for the next attack. The Knights have to get it together because now the entire kingdom is under threat…again. Robin decides he must upgrade the Knights' vehicles and make them more “fight optimized.” Ava and Merlok 2.0 must figure out how to use new NEXO Powers because regular NEXO Powers do make Stone monsters back into stone, but the Cloud simply zaps them to life again. Meanwhile, in the IRON MOUNTAINS, Jestro and the Cloud of Monstrox find the first of the Forbidden Powers: RELENTLESS RUST. They find the ROGUL STATUE holding the power and zap him to life. Now…they can rust those shiny knights to pieces! Ava and Merlok are attempting to use NEXO COMBO POWERS – with hilarious results – as they try to figure out how they might work. When Jestro attacks, he's able to halt the Fortrex by instantly rusting its tracks and wheels! Robin has his hands full trying to clean all the rust from the attack of Relentless Rust. As the Knights attempt to defend against Jestro, the Cloud is zapping statues to life. He hits a statue with a bolt of lightning as Clay is touching it. Something happens…Clay feels odd stiff as if rock is mixing with his body. Finally, Ava and Merlok 2.0 download to the Knights their new thing: a COMBO NEXO COMBO Power. It works! It can smash the animated statues to dust! They use it to defeat Jestro, but he and the Cloud get away with the Forbidden Power of Relentless Rust. Victory … sort of. Clay talks to Merlok 2.0…something has happened to him. What can they do? Merlok 2.0 is very concerned.

programme.title: Thunderstox
programme.desc: Jestro and the Cloud of Monstrox encounters the sculptor. He helps unleash a horde of fierce garden gnomes on Knighton. What will the NEXO KNIGHTS do against this new enemy? Will their new COMBO NEXO Powers do the trick? As Jestro and the Cloud of Monstrox head off on the Evil Mobile to 'store' the Forbidden Power in a secret location, they encounter…THE SCULPTOR. His real name is ROBERTO ARNOLDI. He's a sensitive and odd artist who has been laid off by the king (the King ordered a "smashing" statue of the Queen, and the Sculptor made a statue of the Queen literally smashing monsters. He takes everything very literally.) Jestro instructs him to make a "bad" statue that he can use to attack the knights. The Sculptor sets to work. Meanwhile, the NEXO KNIGHTS are discussing the COMBO NEXO Powers, the Forbidden Powers, and what all this means. Lance, meanwhile, decides that since his talk show career is on hold, he thinks he'll install cameras in the Fortrex and do a Big Brother-like expose show on the Knights (he decides NOT to tell the others.) Merlok 2.0 and the Cloud of Monstrox (each talking to their cohorts) explain the history of the Forbidden Powers: it's a history lesson about the WIZARDS' COUNCIL, the Forbidden Powers, and how they were stopped long ago. This visual montage leads Jestro to…MOUNT THUNDERSTROX. The Cloud of Monstrox shows him the circle of ten Standing Stones where he will insert the Forbidden Powers. "What'll happen after that?” asks Jestro, curious. "Oh, I don't want to spoil the surprise," says the Cloud. They insert the first Forbidden Power, Relentless Rust, and it locks into place on its stone. They go off to battle the Knights and collect the "bad" statue the sculptor is making. But he's made a literally bad statue…it's terrible ... garden gnomes. Like an amateur art student. And…it's not even finished. ("You can't rush bad genius!") The Knights attack with their newly modified vehicles and they succeed in destroying the Evil Mobile. They then try to use a COMBO NEXO Power, but can't…it had to be perfect to work (Ava and Merlok need to work on it.) As Jestro and the Cloud retreat, Merlok again talks to Clay about turning to stone! Merlok calls in Robin…he will need him to build something to help Clay as he becomes more and more impaired.

programme.title: Weekend At Halberts
programme.desc: Party time in Knighton! The mood is down so the King decides to throw a party. In the meantime Jestro attacks the Fortrex, but he is confronted with the new NEXO Power "The Wedgie of Doom". Clay is turned to stone and everyone is gloomy. Especially King Halbert. In order to get everything in a better mood Aaron organizes a celebration. The King wants a reenactment of Clay's heroic rescue of the Queen. He is (as usual) totally unaware of Clay's situation. In order not to disappoint him further, the Knights get a remote control for Clay's Battle suit and is thus able to “perform” as Clay. Lance is happy to exercise his acting abilities and quickly gets into the role of the uptight knight of knights. Ava is working on reinstalling Merlok 2.0 into the Fortrex with Robin. She is also preparing the Fortrex for a visit from the new Knights' Academy novices. It is a fieldtrip with Principal Brickland and Sir Griffiths with the goal of teaching the kids about these new so-called High-tech battle strategies. Not really feeling it for anything modern, it quickly turns into a regular trip back memory lane for Brickland and Sir Griffiths. This out of control excursion is an excellent moment for Jestro who is looking for an opportunity to cheer up the defeated Monstrox. Unfortunately, for Jestro, Ava is able to make Merlok 2.0 functional again, and the Novices are able to fight Jestro and his monsters off using the NEXO Power: Wedgie of Doom. It is a humiliating defeat. Apparently, this is just what the doctor ordered for Monstrox. A good laugh at someone else's expense. His evil mood is back on.

programme.title: Awakening
programme.desc: When Mateo's comic creation, Z-Blob, follows him from a dream back to Brooklyn, Mateo and his sister realize the Dream World is real, and in danger!

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: As tension builds within the Wreckers, they find themselves confronted with the return of the Predacon.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Unlikely duos team up, including Acree and Wheeljack, and Agent Fowler and Jack's mom.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Aerialbots are thrown back in time to Cybertron's Golden Age.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Megatron becomes infected with cosmic rust, a metal-consuming plague.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Megatron has created an electromagnetic life-form. Although tiny and cute, he is also deadly and dangerous.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: After a failed attempt on Megatron's life, Starscream is banished to a deserted island.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: United as Bruticus, the Combaticons are virtually unstoppable.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Aerialbots Slingshot and Skydive disguise themselves as normal jet fighters.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Three high school students create more trouble than they can handle.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: As the Autobots celebrate their victory in the great war, they are attacked by mysterious, evil beings.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Bumblebee faces off against the newest villain, Nanosec.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Bumblebee and Bulkhead are having fun celebrating their first-ever Halloween with Sari.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: Bulkhead realizes that Sari's newest toy is really evil. Unfortunately, none of the Autobots believe him.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The Autobots go face to face with two new Decepticons, Lugnut and Blitzwing.

programme.title: Transformers
programme.desc: The kids try to gather the three Requiem Blaster minicons together.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Smurf Who Couldn't Say No: Being asked to go to the Marsh to bring back the rare Foxfire is a great honor for a Smurf. But Scaredy Smurf is reluctant to confront the Marsh Monster so he sends the Smurf who cannot say no.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Winged Wizard: Determined to fly over Smurf village to catch a Smurf to take to the Wizard's Convention, Gargamel invokes his Book of Spells to help him. A series of funny flying machine mishaps occur and Gargamel is once more the laughing stock of the Wizard's Convention.

programme.title: The First Telesmurf
programme.desc: Papa Smurf pours a magic potion over the new Smurf-melon patch, which instantly grows and grows all over the village and forest. The Smurfs use the blossoms as a telephone, but before Gargamel can trace their voices, the flowers turn into melons and telesmurf is no more.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Hats Off To Smurf: In his search for something new, Vanity dons a Gargamel-made magic hat that transforms his face into a mess. Through trickery, Papa Smurf gets the antidote from the wicked wizard and once more, Vanity is beautiful.

programme.title: No Time For Smurfs
programme.desc: Handy, Brainy, Clumsy, and Smurfette discover the hourglass of time in a cave and due to their bungling, time spins erratically out of control for all of the Smurfs.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Chief Record Smurf: Due to a wild experiment Clumsy is able to store endless knowledge into his memory. Gargamel captures him, trying to unlock Papa Smurf's invisibility formula, but at the last minute, Clumsy tricks him with the wrong ingredients.

programme.title: Good Neighbour Smurf
programme.desc: When Brainy's house burns down, he moves in with his fellow Smurfs and one by one they kick him out because of his meddling.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - A Little Smurf Confidence: Papa Smurf gives Weakling a special cream to wear that will restore his strength. Shortly after, he is awarded for his daring deeds of strength and his confidence really soars when he realizes the cream is smurfberry jam.

programme.title: Harmony Steals The Show
programme.desc: Harmony unwittingly signs a contract with Ghost Writer for a symphony that imprisons him in a ghostly nightclub for life! Papa Smurf demands a jury trial with ghostly jurors and a judge that finds Harmony innocent and Ghost Writer guilty of plagiarism.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Hogatha's Hearththrob: Hogatha the witch promises Gargamel a magical whistle if he will introduce her to the warlock of her dreams. But they are both deceived by each other's trickery: Gargamel dresses up as the warlock, and Hogatha's magical whistle is only a birdcall.

programme.title: A Bell For Azrael
programme.desc: Gargamel puts a spell on a bell attached to Azrael's tail that will lead the cat to the Smurfs. Papa Smurf outwits him with a counter-spell that leads the wizard to Big Mouth instead of the tasty Smurfs.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Smurfette For A Day: Hefty dresses like Smurfette to lure an enamored gnome, Piltkin, away from the Smurf Village. They burrow underground and meet a female gnome who falls in love with Piltkin who then casts Smurfette aside for his new heartthrob.

programme.title: The Magic Earrings
programme.desc: Hogatha manages to obtain the magic golden earrings of Sonoria that will enable her to hear Smurfy voices from afar. Following Lazy's snores, she almost reaches the Smurf Village until Harmony's horn-blasts blow in her earrings and she flies home in a rage.

programme.title: Gargamel's Last Will
programme.desc: Scruple, apprentice to the evil wizard Gargamel, is not above playing a trick or two on his master. When he learns that Puppy, the dog that belongs to the Smurfs, bit Gargamel, Scruple tells the wizard that by midnight, he'll have changed into a dog....

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Dancing Bear: When his bear runs away from his fairground show, the gruff, uncouth Malakoff thinks he's out of business. But he's able to catch a much better show: the Smurflings. Can Brainy and Wild Smurf find a way to bring back the runaway bear?

programme.title: Smurfing The Unicorns
programme.desc: When Puppy, the Smurfs' well-loved dog, drinks tainted water and becomes very sick, Papa Smurf says there is only one chance to save him – but what is needed is the touch of a unicorn's horn. Can the Smurfs find it?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - Nobody Smurf: Nobody Smurf is the Smurf that everybody always forgets. He'd like to develop a personality, get some sort of recognition from his peers, but he fails miserably. Must he remain forever the one that is overlooked? Or will he find his true personality?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Three Smurfketeers: In another Smurf theatrical super-production based – very loosely – on Alexandre Dumas' characters, a masterpiece of the stage is created, with a few lines from Romeo and Juliet thrown in.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Smurfs' Christmas: The Smurfs come to the rescue of two children and their grandfather when a mysterious stranger appears and causes their sleigh to crash. Knocking on Gargamel's door for help puts them and the Smurfs in danger…

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - A Clockwork Smurf: Handy has built a very complex Smurf machine that does everything. It proves dangerous when it nearly destroys the village, but comes in useful when, with the help of the other Smurfs, it saves the young prince Gerard's kingdom from the queen aunt Imperia's dastardly intentions.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Fountain Of Smurf: The Smurfs discover a fountain of youth that is guarded by a wicked troll, who steals Papa Smurf's magic book. They convince Gargamel to help them and the greedy villain runs to get the water, but manages to fall in with the troll. As the two chase each other all over the countryside, the Smurfs are able to retrieve Papa Smurf's book.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs - The Littlest Giant: Simon is lost in the forest and in search of his parents. Simon is the son of a couple of giants. The Smurfs try to bring him home, but then Gargamel interrupts the party.

programme.title: The Rabbids are moving.
programme.desc: A Rabbid has fun playing a game where he suddenly jumps out of a box to surprise his friends.

programme.title: Dr Mad Rabbid is out of control!
programme.desc: Mad Rabbid has found a great way to become invisible, now he just needs a guinea pig to test it.

programme.title: The Rabbids are fed up!
programme.desc: Amazed by a dog scratching himself, the Rabbids approach him, and get the dog's fleas.

programme.title: The Rabbids are escaping!
programme.desc: John the scientist is trapped inside a rabbid's body, and gets thrown into the junkyard.

programme.title: The Rabbids go to the Moon.
programme.desc: To fight against boredom, the rabbids launch a competition of strange noises.

programme.title: The Rabbids land at the North Pole.
programme.desc: Taking pity on a pack of frozen fish, the rabbids decide to set it free and embark on a risky mission.

programme.title: The Rabbids have to be cunning.
programme.desc: After his pals seemingly managed to reach the moon, a rabbid fins himself alone in the junkyard.

programme.title: Rabbids experience.
programme.desc: In a laboratory, the Rabbids are subjected to tests designed to determine just how idiotic they really are.

programme.title: The Rabbids turn the city upside down.
programme.desc: The Rabbids get their hands on a remote-controlled car, and figure out how to use the controller.

programme.title: The Rabbids have a great plan.
programme.desc: Four rabbids find a wooden horse and tries to make it move by all means, while drawing Zak's attention.

programme.title: The Rabbids are uncontrollable.
programme.desc: An alien accidentally finds himself in the Junkyard among the rabbids.

programme.title: The Rabbids take off.
programme.desc: The rabbids unintentionally help Zak and his childhood crush Zoey develop a romance…

programme.title: The Rabbids turn the city upside down.
programme.desc: The Rabbids get their hands on a remote-controlled car, and figure out how to use the controller.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: To get his friends back from Walter's group, Dennis challenges Walter to a videogames battle. Followed by 'Fish Tale'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: For a school project, Dennis and Walter both build robots. However, during an accident, both robots merge, causing Dennis and Walter to defend the school. Followed by 'No Joke.'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: With one backstage pass for a Ratbucket gig, Dennis and his friends pass themselves off as Ratbucket, only for the real Ratbucket not to be able to play at his own concert. Followed by 60 second Dennis and 'Bad Dad'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After Dennis breaks his bike, Dad is unable to afford a new one, so Dennis convinces him to ask his boss for a raise. Unfortunately, Mr Scrimp reduces his wages instead. Dennis then trashes his house to convince Scrimp that his family are poor, so that Mr Scrimp will feel compelled to raise Dad's wages. Just as Scrimp has been taken in by Dennis' plan, Mum and Dad arrive home laden with shopping bags, resulting in Dad getting fired on the spot. Dennis resolves to get Dad his old job back by secretly causing chaos at his office. To restore order Scrimp rehires Dad, sealing the deal with a generous pay rise. Mum and Dad repair the house, but still don't have enough money to buy Dennis a new bike, instead it gets repaired with Dad's office rubber bands and paperclips. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Dennis Hiccups'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Believing Gnasher and Gnipper to be trapped in Beanotown Dog Pound, Dennis comes up with a plan to rescue his friends and bring them both home. Followed by more Dennis and Gnasher episodes.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: When Dennis breaks his skateboard again, Dad agrees to buy Dennis the best skateboard there is as a replacement if he is able to win a prize at the upcoming school award ceremony. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Dance of the Seven Pies'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis tries to get a birthday present by saying thank you to his relatives. However, because of Dennis' naughty personality, they take his attempts in a negative way. Followed by 'Dennis Hiccups'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: To get his friends back from Walter's group, Dennis challenges Walter to a videogames battle. Followed by 60 Second Dennis and 'Masters of the House'.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: After a wrestling accident, Gnasher swallows a canister of laughing gas at the vet's. Dennis, along with Gnasher hide in the conference centre, where dad gives a speech. However, the canister leaks, causing Gnasher to fart laughing gas and having everyone laughing. Followed by 'Attack of the Walterbot'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis and Co. have to control Pie Face's obsession with pies during the Pie Festival. Otherwise, they will be grounded. Followed by 'Great Outdoors'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: On a school trip, Dennis finds out that he is descentant to a warrior tribe. He also discovers that Montanna Smith (a famous Indiana Jones-esque explorer and one of Walter's personal heroes) is actually a thief who's trying to steal the treasure of Dennis' ancestor. Will he be stopped before he gets his hands on the treasure? Followed by Dog Tired.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Mum has bought curtain material for the soon to be newly decorated kitchen, and asks Dad to look after it while she is out. However, Dad falls asleep and mistakenly agrees that Dennis can use it as a giant sail. When Dad realises that it has gone, he calls Dennis to bring it back, but unfortunately Dennis gets into a spot of bother with the colonel, who takes the curtain material off Dennis. In an attempt to steal it back, the garden being wrecked and the kitchen is left looking like a bombsite. Dennis, Gnasher and Dad have to clean up the whole kitchen and redecorate it in time for Mum returning. When Mum decides it is time to hang the new curtains, Dennis is about to get the curtain material, but Mum reveals that she has changed her mind and bought new curtains instead. Followed by 'Fangs for the Memories'

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
programme.desc: Dennis, his friends and Walter's friends get marooned on a desert island when Dennis attempts to escape a boring field trip. Followed by Substitute Creecher

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher
